title: ProviderKeyRouterState
description: Schema for local provider key routing decisions. Captures the routing state that maps tasks to provider credentials based on availability, capability, cooldown, and failover policy. Written by the key router before worker dispatch; read by the launcher and monitoring. See docs/ai-native/provider-key-router.md for architecture and field-level documentation. Never contains secret values.

schemaVersion.description: Schema version pinned to 1. Consumers must reject records with a different version.

capturedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when this routing snapshot was captured.

selectionStrategy.description: Algorithm used to select among available routes. least-loaded = pick the provider with the most remaining capacity. round-robin = rotate through available providers. failover = use providers in priority order, fall through on exhaustion. manual = operator selects provider explicitly.

dryRun.description: When true, the router computes routing decisions but does not dispatch workers. All write-capable automation defaults to dry-run unless explicitly overridden by an operator.

routes.description: Active routing entries. Each route maps a provider credential to its current routing eligibility, cooldown state, and failover priority.

failoverChain.description: Ordered list of provider ids defining the failover sequence. The router tries providers in this order when the primary is exhausted or at capacity. Must reference provider ids present in routes.

route.description: A single provider routing entry. Links a provider credential to its routing eligibility and current state.

providerId.description: Stable provider identifier. Must match entries in provider-pool-policy.json, provider-pool.json, and provider-secret-ref.schema.json.

secretRefSource.description: Source type for the credential backing this route. Matches sourceType in provider-secret-ref.schema.json. The router uses this to resolve the actual key at dispatch time — the key value itself is never stored.

secretRefKey.description: Lookup key within the source (e.g. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY for env-var, credential target name for credential-manager). Matches sourceKey in provider-secret-ref.schema.json. This is a pointer, not a value.

isRoutable.description: Whether this route is currently eligible for new task dispatch. False when the provider is exhausted, disabled, at capacity, or in cooldown.

unroutableReason.description: Why this route is not routable. null when isRoutable is true. exhausted = quota/rate limit hit. disabled = manual or auth-failure disable. at-capacity = maxConcurrency reached. cooldown = waiting for cooldown to expire. auth-failure = credential rejected (401/403).

priority.description: Routing priority. Lower numbers are tried first in failover mode. Ties are broken by selection strategy. Must be unique within the routes array when strategy is failover.

capabilities.description: API capabilities this credential supports. The router only routes tasks to providers whose capabilities match the task requirements.

currentConcurrency.description: Number of workers currently routed to this provider.

maxConcurrency.description: Maximum concurrent workers this route can serve. Must match the corresponding policy entry.

cooldownExpiresAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the cooldown period for this route ends. Null when not in cooldown. 15 min for rate-limit (429), 60 min for quota exhaustion.

lastFailureClass.description: Classification of the last failure on this route. null if no failures. exhaustion = quota/rate limit. auth = credential rejected. runtime = transient provider error.

consecutiveFailures.description: Number of consecutive failures on this route. Reset to 0 on successful dispatch.

lastRoutedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last task routed to this provider. Null if never routed.

globalRoutingSummary.description: Aggregate routing metrics for launch gate and monitoring consumption.

totalRoutable.description: Count of routes where isRoutable is true.

totalActiveWorkers.description: Sum of currentConcurrency across all routes.

globalMaxWorkers.description: Hard ceiling on total concurrent workers. Must match concurrency.globalMaxWorkers in provider-pool-policy.json.

lastUpdatedBy.description: Identifier of the script or process that last wrote this file (e.g. 'provider-key-router.ps1').
